scenarios.title: New user registration with valid credentials
scenarios.description: A new visitor submits a valid email and password to create an account. The system creates the user record, hashes the password, and returns a JWT token for immediate dashboard access.
scenarios.expected_behavior: HTTP 201 response with JWT token in body; user record created in database; UserSettings record created with defaults; no password stored in plaintext
pass_criteria: Response status is 201
Response body contains token field with valid JWT
User record exists in database with hashed password
UserSettings record created with notifications_enabled = true
Duplicate registration with same email returns 409

scenarios.title: Registered user login with correct credentials
scenarios.description: An existing user submits their email and password. The system validates credentials against the stored hash and issues a JWT token for session use.
scenarios.expected_behavior: HTTP 200 response with JWT token; token payload contains userId and expiry set to 24 hours from issuance; invalid credentials return 401 without leaking account existence
pass_criteria: Response status is 200 for valid credentials
JWT token is returned in response body
Token decodes to contain userId matching the registered user
Token expiry is set to 24 hours from issuance
Invalid password returns 401 — not 403 or 500
Non-existent email returns 401 — same response as invalid password

scenarios.title: Password reset flow completes end to end
scenarios.description: A user who has forgotten their password requests a reset link, receives it via email, clicks the link within the validity window, and successfully sets a new password.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Reset link delivered to registered email; link expires after 1 hour; new password replaces old hash; old password no longer valid after reset; new login succeeds with new password
pass_criteria: POST /auth/reset-request returns 200 for registered email (no account existence leak)
Reset token created in database with 1-hour expiry
Email sent to registered address (verified via SendGrid mock)
POST /auth/reset-complete with valid token and new password returns 200
Old password rejected after reset
New password accepted for login after reset
Expired token returns 410 Gone

scenarios.title: Authenticated user sees populated dashboard within 2 seconds
scenarios.description: A logged-in user navigates to the dashboard. All widgets load with current data and the page becomes interactive within the 2-second performance budget.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Dashboard renders with all configured widgets; metrics widgets show current counts; activity feed shows last 10 events; page interactive within 2 seconds on standard broadband
pass_criteria: GET /dashboard returns 200 with full widget data payload
Response time is under 200ms at p95 in staging
Frontend Time to Interactive (TTI) under 2000ms measured by Lighthouse
All widgets render without console errors
Authenticated request without valid JWT returns 401

scenarios.title: Widget layout persists across logout and login
scenarios.description: A user reorders their dashboard widgets, logs out, and logs back in. The customized layout is restored without any re-configuration.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Layout saved on reorder; layout retrieved on next login; widget positions match the user's last saved configuration exactly
pass_criteria: POST /dashboard/layout saves the layout to UserSettings
Subsequent GET /dashboard returns the saved layout
Layout is user-specific — different users see their own layouts
Layout persists across token refresh and re-login
Invalid layout payload returns 422 with validation errors

feature_gates.gate: All SC-AUTH-001 through SC-AUTH-003 must pass before F2 implementation begins
All SC-DASH-001 through SC-DASH-002 must pass before F3 implementation begins
